Manchester City are enjoying another strong campaign in 2025/26, having already lifted the Carabao Cup and reached the FA Cup Final, while remaining firmly in the Premier League title race as they pursue a domestic treble.
Up front, Erling Haaland is once again leading the charge, currently topping the Premier League Golden Boot race with 24 goals to his name so far this season.
Pep Guardiola’s side still have three home league fixtures remaining at the Etihad Stadium against Brentford, Crystal Palace and Aston Villa on the final day of the season.
With our official Manchester City hospitality packages, you can experience world-class football at the Etihad Stadium as the season reaches its final stage.

Manchester City Managers Corner
Manager’s Corner is inspired to celebrate some of Manchester City’s great former managers. Everything from the interiors to the excellent service experienced at Etihad Stadium, Manager’s Corner delivers Man City fans a memorable matchday experience. A place to enjoy authentic Mediterranean food and drink, you have the option to purchase delicious pizzas from the pizza oven, charcuterie small plates, plus the finest wines and craft ales. VIP Seats in the South West corner ensure a stunning panoramic view of Etihad’s pitch, making Manager’s Corner a popular package for all Man City fans.
Man City Manager’s Corner Package at Etihad includes:
Access to the exclusive Managers Corner Bar
A premium seat in the South West Corner to watch Man City
Option to purchase a selection of pizzas and other small plates
Complimentary half-time tea and coffee

Etihad Stadium – Manchester City Hospitality Dress Code
At the Etihad Stadium, guests are wanted to feel that ‘warm welcome’ from the staff. The dress code for Man City home games at the Etihad Stadium is smart casual, which is great for those in need of an action-packed day. The Etihad Stadium strictly prohibits:
Tracksuits
Caps
Shorts
Offensive clothing and away tops are strictly prohibited
However, the clothing listed below is acceptable
Jeans
Smart, clean trainers
Home jersey tops
Please be aware that these rules do not apply to children under the age of 12 years old.
Manchester City Football Club – Premier League
Pep Guardiola’s Manchester City finished 3rd in the  Premier League last term. They finished last season strongly on 71 points and were unbeaten in their final nine games of the campaign.
Pep Guardiola has been City’s Head Coach since succeeding Manuel Pellegrini on 1st July 2016.
With our official Manchester City hospitality packages, you can enjoy the world-class football City has to offer at the Etihad Stadium. From VIP seating offering prime views of the pitch to Champagne receptions, Man City hospitality allows you to enjoy matchday in style!
Manchester City welcomes Sky Blue fans to the Etihad Stadium as they look to mount a challenge to retain their title of Premier League Champions and to lift the Carabao Cup once again this term, with Pep Guardiola’s team looking to have yet another trophy-laden campaign this season.
Man City won the Premier League last in the 2023/24 season, finishing top of the Premiership with 91 points, 2 points clear of second-place Arsenal. Of the 38 games played last season, they won 21, drew 8, and lost 9. Their home form at Etihad Stadium towards the end of the season was was very impressive.
Erling Haaland was their leading scorer in the Premier League last season, netting no less than 22 goals from 31 appearances. The Norwegian forward installs fear into most Premier League defences with displays of football that are the envy of most club managers and players.
Manchester City Transfers
Pep Guardiola’s Manchester City made a number of signings in the 2025 Summer Transfer Window, ahead of the start of the 2025/26 Premier League season. They signed Dutch midfielder Tijjani Reijnders from AC Milan for a reported £46.5 million, whilst they also sealed a deal for Wolves left back Rayan Ait-Nouri, who they were able to sign from the West Midlands club for a reported fee of an initial £31 million, plus £5.3 million in add-ons as well.
Not only that, City also signed Olympique Lyonnais star Rayan Cherki, with the attacking midfielder having joined from the French club for a fee that was reported to be £34 million. Norwegian midfielder Sverre Nypan also joined from Rosenborg for what was a rumoured fee of £12.5 million. Chelsea Goalkeeper Marcus Bettinelli also signed for the club for what was a nominal fee.
James Trafford, who came through the City Academy, also signed, re-joining the club from newly promoted Burnley for a rumoured transfer fee of £27 million, with him signing a five year contract that is set to expire in June 2030.
In the January 2026 Transfer Window, City signed two players. The first player signed by Pep Guardiola and his side was Bournemouth forward Antoine Semenyo, who joined from their fellow Premier League club for a rumoured transfer fee of £62.5 million, whilst England central defender Marc Guehi signed from London club Crystal Palace for what was a rumoured transfer fee of £20 million. The two players both agreed and signed five and a half year contracts with the club.

Description
After 80 years at their previous home stadium (Maine Road), Manchester City moved to Etihad Stadium in 2003. Before becoming the Etihad Stadium as we know it today, the venue functioned as an athletics stadium and hosted the Commonwealth Games in 2002.
Manchester City History
Manchester City Football Club was founded in 1880 as St. Mark’s (West Gorton) and adopted the name Manchester City in 1894. The English Football Club entered the League in 1899 and had its first major period of success in the 1960s. Man City’s historical logo includes a ship (to symbolise the historical trade function of the city) and the red rose of Lancaster (symbolising their historical connection with Lancashire). Meanwhile, their popular nickname – The Sky Blues – originates from the colour of their kits – Cambridge blue and sky blue.
Manchester City Hospitality Tickets & Packages  – FAQs
What are the dates for 2025/26 Premier League season?
The 2025/26 Premier League season dates are Saturday, 16 August 2025 – Sunday, 24 May 2026. Changes to fixtures will be updated on Eventmasters’ site in the Fixtures List above.
The fixtures for the 2025/26 Premier League season will be announced on Wednesday, June 18, at 9am BST
When do season tickets go on sale for 2025/26 season?
Tickets will be sold through staggered sales windows, starting with those who have purchased the highest number of matches.
What are Man City season ticket prices for the 2025/26 season?
Prices for Man City season tickets vary by stand.
General admission Season Tickets and Premier League match-by-match ticket prices will be frozen for the 2025/26 season
Do I need to be a season ticket holder to buy VIP Hospitality Packages?
To buy our VIP Hospitality Packages, you do NOT need to be a season ticket holder.
I am travelling from abroad can I buy Man City hospitality packages from you?
INTERNATIONAL / OVERSEAS MAN CITY FANS – Yes, we do accommodate overseas visitors and have a large number of clients from all over the United States. Man City fans living abroad can buy English Premier League tickets for Manchester City matches from all over the world. Just pick your chosen Man City soccer game and check out online, ensuring that your card details are correct and that the ‘billing address’ used for the transaction matches the address to that your card is registered. Our payment portal is very secure, and the ‘billing address’ and ‘card registered’ addresses must match for the payment to go through. Alternatively, if you select ‘Telephone Payments’ when checking out online, our sales team will call you and verify your tickets and take payment over the telephone.
How do I keep up to date with fixture rescheduling?
Information on confirmed fixture changes is included in the Publications section of the Manchester City website.
What should I wear to Man City Hospitality?
Hospitality areas at Manchester City operate a smart-casual dress code at the Etihad Stadium. Most facilities do not allow shorts, tracksuits, away colours, caps, or sportswear.
How can I make an enquiry about booking?
Call us on 0121 233 6500. Or fill out our contact form, and we will get in touch as soon as possible.
When do I get the tickets? Do I get them immediately after the order?
Match tickets are digital and sent via email. This is usually 3-5 days prior to each match day.
Does The Etihad Stadium have wheelchair access?
With its modern design, the Etihad Stadium is widely respected as one of the easiest stadiums to access in a wheelchair, with accessible bays in all four stands.
How much is a private box at Man City?
10 Seater Boxes are available on a match-by-match basis for selected fixtures and cost in the region of £1,850 per person. If you wish to enquire about a Box at Etihad Stadium, please call our sales team on 0121 233 6500, who will be happy to help you.
How much is Manchester City Tunnel Club?
How much is Manchester City Tunnel Club? – Tunnel Club Prices for Manchester City games at Etihad Stadium vary depending on the fixture. See our fixture list below. Once you have selected your fixture, just click the BOOK NOW button, and this will take you to the fixture page, where all the prices are clearly laid out for each fixture. If you need any help with booking the Tunnel Club or any of the other facilities at Etihad Stadium, please call our sales team on 0121 233 6500, and our Man City ticketing team will help you.

Frequently Asked Questions
What is Man City Hospitality?
The official hospitality packages at Manchester City are a step up from normal tickets. Manchester City hospitality tickets typically include a range of premium services designed to enhance the matchday experience for fans. While specific offerings can vary depending on the package, here are some common elements you might find in Man City hospitality tickets: Premium seating, meal before the game, complimentary drinks, match programme and Q&A from some Man City legends. We have several packages such as Tunnel Club, Chairmans and the more informal 9320 suite. Please ask for our range of official Manchester City VIP hospitality ticket options.
How much is a VIP box at Man City?
The cost of a private box at the Etihad Stadium varies on the game. For example a box to see the Manchester derby or their last home game of the season would be a lot more than a home League Cup game. Boxes come with hostess, complimentary bar, pre match meal and seats directly outside your box. Man City boxes tend to open three hours prior to kick off. If there is a certain game, you are interested in feel free to email us and we will send over prices and inclusions.
What is the dress code for Man City Hospitality?
The dress code for the hospitality lounges insist on no tracksuits, caps, football shirts or ripped jeans. The dress code does not apply to children under 12 who can wear Man City football tops.
What does Man City hospitality include?
The inclusions vary from package to package but as a rough guide please see the below.
VIP Seating: Some of the best seats in the stadium, often middle tier padded seats alongside the pitch
Exclusive Lounge Access: Entry to luxurious hospitality lounges or private suites where guests can relax before, during, and after the match.
Fine Dining: Gourmet dining options, including seven course meals, fine wines, and premium beverages. Some packages offer a la carte menus, while others include buffets or themed dining experiences.
Complimentary Drinks: Access to a range of complimentary alcoholic and non-alcoholic beverages.
Entertainment: Pre-match entertainment such as live music, guest speakers, or appearances by club legends.
Match-day Programmes: Complimentary official matchday programmes and team sheets.
Personalised Service: Dedicated service from the hostesses, ensuring a seamless and enjoyable experience.
VIP Parking: Reserved parking spaces close to the stadium.
Exclusive Merchandise: Access to exclusive merchandise or discounts at the club shop.
Behind-the-Scenes Tours: Some packages may include behind-the-scenes tours of the stadium, such as visits to the players’ tunnel, dugouts, or changing rooms.

Don’t Just Take Our Word For It
VIP Tickets FAQ’s:
Not sure about the purchasing process or what to do on the day? Check out these common questions!
How will my tickets be delivered?
+
Tickets will normally be sent 72 hours prior to your event. These are all E-tickets and will be sent to the email you registered on your booking. Please check your tickets as soon as possible and notify us if they do not match what you have ordered.  Together with your tickets will be a confirmation of the experience you have purchased, and an itinerary for the day. Your tickets will not be sent without cleared payment.
If you need to change your email delivery details, please log in to your account, or contact us at bookings@circuithospitality.com  or +44(0) 1454 201 210.
Will my seats be sat together?
+
If you buy multiple tickets, we guarantee that your seats will be located together in the stadium.
I am buying the packages as a gift; can I get a voucher to present?
+
If you are purchasing the packages as a gift and require a voucher to give to the recipient, we can arrange this.  Please email bookings@circuithospitality.com with your request, including your order number and name, and what you would like as a message on the voucher, and will email over a voucher you can print out.
What is included in the cost?
+
Everything included in a package is listed under the package details. Every Premier League football hospitality experience includes a ticket to watch the game as well as a variety of other elements, which could include dining experiences, complimentary bars, match programmes, and more. If you have any questions about what is included in a specific package, then do not hesitate to contact a member of the Circuit team.
What happens when matches move for TV broadcast?
+
The dates for some Premier League football matches will move to accommodate television coverage, cup qualification or European competitions. When you make a booking, you are booking the match, not the date, and your ticket will be valid for the new fixture date. Do not hesitate to get in touch if you’d like confirmation of whether a fixture has been moved but consider this when booking any travel arrangements.
Are the packages sold by Circuit Hospitality genuine?
+
Circuit Hospitality is the largest authorised reseller of Premier League hospitality in the UK. Every package listed for sale on our website is owned and controlled by Circuit Hospitality. We have been selling Premier League hospitality for more than 18 years and has long standing relationships with a large percentage of the Premier League teams. We own most packages seasonally and are often able to offer these on a match-by-match basis.

The 2024-25 season was an underwhelming one for Pep Guardiola’s Man City team, as they finished trophyless and could only reach third position in the Premier League. Despite the loss of their talisman, Kevin De Bruyne, their squad is packed full of talent, and it will be no surprise to see them competing for the top honours in the game come May.
